symptom: cramping belly pain and vomiting that would not stop for days
hook: the bowel was stretched into huge loops that just stopped cold at one exact point
whatYouSee: Loops of small bowel stretch far wider than normal with visible fluid levels then the bowel suddenly returns to normal size at one point.
whyItMatters: Something is physically blocking the intestine often scar tissue from an old surgery and everything upstream of that point is backing up.
treatment: Many cases settle with a tube to decompress the bowel and fluids while a persistent block needs surgery to clear it.
takeaway: Dilated loops that suddenly narrow at one spot mark exactly where the blockage sits.
laypersonQuestion: Does that middle section look too swollen to you?
seedHint: Follow the loops and see exactly where they suddenly go back to normal size.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with cramping belly pain and vomiting that would not stop for days.

Then the CT scan loaded 😭

And the bowel was stretched into huge loops that just stopped cold at one exact point.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Small bowel obstruction

👀 What you see:
Loops of small bowel stretch far wider than normal with visible fluid levels then the bowel suddenly returns to normal size at one point.

🦴 Why it matters:
Something is physically blocking the intestine often scar tissue from an old surgery and everything upstream of that point is backing up.

💊 Treatment:
Many cases settle with a tube to decompress the bowel and fluids while a persistent block needs surgery to clear it.

If you like guessing what is wrong on an X-ray wait until you see it on a CT scan.

I put 10 real CT cases into Weird CT Case Files: Volume 1.
